title: Setting template projects with time intervals
excerpt: We start onboarding projects on a regular basis that normally take the same amount of time and are in a specific order. It would save us SO much time if you could design the software to allow us add a transition date and…

posts.author: wjohnson_oneascent (Wade Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml: We start onboarding projects on a regular basis that normally take the same amount of time and are in a specific order. It would save us SO much time if you could design the software to allow us add a transition date and then allow us to set each step to a certain number of days before or after that transition day. We are able to set them to a specific date currently, but that makes it so confusing to keep up with the overall project as we make updates to our template.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @wjohnson_oneascent

Welcome to the community! Actually you can do this but it requires a few additional steps. It is called workspace templates and you need to build a little app for that. These template has the concept of a “date anchor” that serves your needs. See https://apps.developer.monday.com/docs/workspace-templates for a step-by-step walkthrough.
